mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending power and corporate earnings growth, driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in greater than 65 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, invalidating the labor cooling signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad consumer demand contraction, directly reducing corporate revenue forecasts and equity prices.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST yield positive 20-day forward returns in greater than 60 percent of observed cases, refuting the demand contraction mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding two percent annualized signals capacity utilization tightening that triggers inflation expectations and equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 2.0 percent correlates with positive 5-day returns in greater than 55 percent of observations, negating the capacity constraint signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a flat federal funds rate creates negative real yields, prompting institutional investors to rotate capital out of equities into inflation hedges.
failure_condition: Periods of rising CPIAUCSL with flat FEDFUNDS show positive 60-day forward returns in greater than 60 percent of cases, disproving the real rate rotation effect.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth expands corporate profit margins and dividend payout capacity, providing fundamental support for equity valuations and positive forward returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year sample, invalidating the fundamental earnings support channel.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment additions exceeding two hundred thousand in a single month signal robust economic activity that triggers short-covering rallies and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Months with payroll additions above 200,000 yield negative 5-day returns in greater than 50 percent of cases, refuting the short-covering rally mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate margins and depress equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO and UNRATE divergence yields positive 20-day forward returns in greater than 65 percent of observations, disproving the margin compression hypothesis.
